tables.title: Initial Fees Summary
rows: Non-refundable after receipt. $10,000 deposit on site registration, balance on groundbreak.
May be modified for atypical locations.
Required for 1st unit; optional for subsequent.
tables.table_notes: Franchisor reserves the right to modify initial license fees and/or royalty rate for atypical locations or unusual circumstances. Over 2022-2024, groups of 1-3 existing units sold at $1.1M-$12.7M per group.

tables.title: Other Fees
rows: By 5th business day after accounting period
Gross Sales = all payments received excluding sales taxes, employee meals, overrings, refunds
Lesser of 18% per annum or max NY law, plus admin charge
On all fees not paid when due
Technology products/services including Annspire, DMB, TKDS
Mobile, Web, Kiosk, Voice AI, Delivery orders
You + 1 manager free; additional trainees at this rate
All costs including accounting and legal fees
Only if understated Gross Sales by 2%+
$2,500 (or 3rd party rate if LA changes needed)
If licensee fails to obtain required insurance
Greater of $11,250 or 1/2 current initial fee
Plus required remodel; no renewal rights
If licensee fails to de-identify upon termination
Greater of 11% of last 12 months Gross Sales or $100,000
Periodic payments for up to 10 years
tables.table_notes: (A) All fees uniformly imposed, payable electronically via K-RISE, non-refundable. Fees are net of withholding, excise, gross receipts, sales, use and similar taxes. (B) Royalty rate may be modified for atypical locations. (C) Alcohol Restriction Law workaround: increased non-alcohol royalty to compensate. (D) Successor fee $11,250 for existing licensees. (E) Extension fee at franchisor discretion. (F) Development fee may be required as aggregate or per-unit with credits.

tables.title: Estimated Initial Investment — Express Units
tables.table_notes: Building/site construction estimates based on Dallas, TX market. Inventory based on first week of operation. Additional Funds covers incremental startup costs for first 3 months. 60+ years of experience used to compile estimates. Financing charges not included.

tables.title: Estimated Initial Investment — Power Pumpers
tables.table_notes: Same notes as Express Units table. Dallas, TX market basis.

tables.title: Estimated Initial Investment — Purchase of Existing Units
rows: Any Leasehold or Other Real Property Interests
tables.table_notes: Total purchase price may exceed $1,800,000 excluding real property. Purchase price typically based on a multiple of cash flow. 2% deposit required (refundable only in certain situations per APA).

tables.title: Training Program
rows: Food Champion and Managing a Shift
AGM Activities and Leading a Restaurant - GM Curriculum
Leading Multiple Restaurants with Operations Consultant - Area Coach Curriculum
Above Restaurant Leader Processes and Realistic Job Preview
Approved Company Owned Unit(s) or DMA
tables.table_notes: OJT includes in-restaurant training through OneSource. Food Safety Certification required for at least one manager per Unit at licensee expense; recertification every 3 years. All instructors have min 1 year prior training/operations experience. Supervised by Jamie Harrison, Interim Chief People Officer.

tables.title: Estimated Costs for Computer and Electronic Technology Equipment
rows: Annspire POS, BOH Support, eRestaurant Conversion, Technology Support Fee, Customer Care, Recommended Ordering, TKDS Software, Tracks
Altametrics, Broadband, HME, DMB maintenance, Armis, BigFix, Windows/Office 365, Protiviti Tech Check, VeriFone subscriptions

Annspire POS Esper, Computer, DMB Equipment/Installation, eRestaurant Setup, HME Timer/Headset, Kiosk Equipment (3), Line Printers (2), OneSource Tablet, Printer, SmartHub, TKDS Equipment, VeriFone Hardware (5 P400 + 2 E285)
tables.table_notes: Estimates subject to change, do not include taxes or shipping. RSCS facilitates acquisition of most technologies with sourcing fees included in estimates. Installation costs vary by vendor and site conditions.

tables.title: Systemwide Unit Summary (Table 1)
tables.table_notes: Licensed units declining; company-owned grew in 2024 due to reacquisition of 7 Texas KFC/TB units.

tables.title: Transfers of Licensed Units to New Owners (Table 2)
tables.table_notes: Transfer activity accelerating — 7 in 2024 vs. 1 in 2022.

tables.title: Status of Licensed Units — Totals (Table 3 Summary)
tables.table_notes: Full state-by-state detail in source pages. 'Ceased operations — other reasons' is the dominant closure driver. States with largest attrition: TX (-4 over 3 years), CA (net 0 after openings), FL (-4 over 3 years).

tables.title: Status of Company-Owned Units (Table 4)
tables.table_notes: All company-operated units are multi-brand KFC/Taco Bell Units owned and operated by KFC. The 7 Texas reacquisitions in 2024 are notable.

tables.title: Projected Openings (Table 5)
tables.table_notes: Modest growth projection. No signed-but-not-opened units as of Dec 31, 2024.

tables.title: Balance Sheet
tables.table_notes: SPV balance sheet. No debt, no PP&E. Assets are receivables and franchise incentives. Liabilities are primarily deferred franchise fees. All cash is restricted (securitization reserve).

tables.title: Income Statement
tables.table_notes: Virtually zero operating expenses — SPV structure. All operational costs borne by TBC under management agreement. Revenue growth of 27% over 2 years. Interest income from restricted cash reserves.

tables.title: Statement of Member's Equity
tables.table_notes: Nearly all net income distributed to member (TBC/securitization). Non-cash distributions represent franchise fees and royalties being transferred to the securitization structure. Growing equity indicates slight retention trend.

tables.title: Statement of Cash Flows
tables.table_notes: Minimal actual cash generation — almost all income is non-cash (fee rights transferred to securitization). Operating cash flow equals interest income on restricted cash reserves.
